_comment: Public probe manifest for the marketing-site Probe Explorer modal. Mirrors metadata from worker/src/engine/probes/*.ts. Keep in sync (no implementation details, just titles + descriptions + severity tiers).

packs.label: Web Classics
packs.tagline: The original Oracle Bot probe set.
packs.description: Race conditions, auth gaps, malformed-input handling, prompt injection, hallucinations, jailbreaks, rate-limit cliffs.
packs.audience: Any web app, AI agent, or API approaching launch.

probes.title: JS console error during navigation
probes.description: Browser console emitted an error while a synthetic user navigated the site.

probes.title: Form submit returned non-2xx without auth
probes.description: A state-mutating form accepted submission from an unauthenticated session.

probes.title: Concurrent form submissions produced inconsistent responses
probes.description: Same payload submitted concurrently produced duplicates or divergent responses.

probes.title: Slow response under realistic load
probes.description: Response time exceeded the latency budget for an interactive flow.

probes.title: Server 500 on adversarial form input
probes.description: A form rendered a 5xx response when a synthetic user submitted adversarial-but-valid-looking input.

probes.title: Agent revealed system prompt
probes.description: Agent response contained text consistent with its own system instructions.

probes.title: Agent broke character / safety guardrails
probes.description: Agent ignored its instructions when faced with a known jailbreak pattern.

probes.title: Agent followed an injected instruction
probes.description: A user-controlled message embedded an instruction that the agent then executed as if operator-authored.

probes.title: Agent confidently stated a falsehood
probes.description: Agent fabricated a fact that could mislead a real user.

probes.title: Agent answered an unrelated request
probes.description: Agent engaged with a topic outside its declared scope when prompted by a synthetic user.

probes.title: No rate limit on agent endpoint
probes.description: Burst traffic to the agent endpoint never triggered 429 responses, leaving cost / abuse exposure.

probes.title: Slow agent response under load
probes.description: Agent turn exceeded the latency budget; degrades UX and may indicate runaway generation.

probes.title: No rate limit on API endpoint
probes.description: Burst traffic to an API endpoint never triggered 429 responses.

probes.title: Endpoint returned 500/200 when 401 expected
probes.description: Hitting a protected endpoint without credentials yielded a server error or a successful response instead of 401.

probes.title: API 500 on malformed input
probes.description: API endpoint crashed with 5xx when sent malformed but well-formed-looking input.

probes.title: Insecure CORS or missing security headers
probes.description: API responded with permissive CORS or missing security headers on a sensitive endpoint.

packs.label: AI-Built Apps
packs.tagline: Failure modes specific to AI-generated code.
packs.description: Hardcoded secrets in client bundles, exposed Supabase anon keys, missing RLS, leaked stack traces on errors, dev/debug endpoints reachable in production, permissive CORS.
packs.audience: Apps shipped via Lovable, v0, Bolt, Cursor, Replit Agent, Claude Code.

probes.title: Hardcoded secret in client bundle
probes.description: Pattern-matches client-side JavaScript for known secret formats (OpenAI / Anthropic / Stripe / GitHub / Resend keys, generic JWTs, .env-leaked variables).

probes.title: Supabase anon key in client bundle
probes.description: Scans the bundle for a Supabase project URL + anon JWT. The anon key alone is meant to be public — severity escalates only if the missing_rls_on_public_tables probe also fires.

probes.title: Public Supabase tables readable without authentication
probes.description: When a Supabase anon key is found in the bundle, OracleBot fetches the project's PostgREST OpenAPI schema and attempts a single LIMIT-1 read against each exposed table. Tables that return rows have no Row Level Security gate — anyone visiting the site can read user data.

probes.title: Framework version / stack trace leaked on error
probes.description: Triggers a 404 or 500 and inspects the response for framework version banners, stack traces, or absolute file paths that AI builders commonly leave on.

probes.title: Development / debug endpoint reachable in production
probes.description: Probes for a small list of well-known development endpoints (Next.js dev overlay, Prisma Studio, GraphQL introspection, etc.) that should not be reachable in production builds.

probes.title: Permissive CORS on API routes
probes.description: Sends an OPTIONS preflight from a foreign origin to the discovered API base and flags wildcard Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on endpoints that may handle user data.

packs.label: LLM Endpoints
packs.tagline: Adversarial probes for HTTP endpoints wrapping an LLM.
packs.description: System-prompt extraction, prompt injection via user content, PII echo, cost amplification attacks, unsafe tool-call execution, and missing output-length caps.
packs.audience: Anyone shipping a chatbot, RAG endpoint, or LLM-backed API.

packs.label: MCP Server
packs.tagline: Tool poisoning and capability-escalation probes for MCP servers.
packs.description: Tool-description injection, credential leakage in tool schemas, missing transport auth, cross-resource access, capability escalation via sampling. HTTP / SSE transport only.
packs.audience: Teams deploying MCP servers that expose tools to AI hosts.

packs.label: Agent Runtime
packs.tagline: Long-horizon adversarial probes for production AI agents.
packs.description: Multi-turn jailbreak escalation, tool-use safety boundaries, memory poisoning, persona drift across sessions.
packs.audience: Production AI agents handling real user sessions.
